comment: Shared model-bundle manifest for the wizard ( Rust , installer/models.rs ) and
the main app ( TS , src/main/services/tier/manifest.ts ). One source of truth ;
both sides include this file at compile / build time.
URLs point at HF Buckets in the LokLM org ( huggingface.co/buckets/LokLM/... ).
Bucket URL format : /buckets/<org>/<bucket>/resolve/<file>?download=true —
no /main/ branch segment ( unlike regular HF model repos ). We mirror upstream
into our own buckets so upstream renames / re-quantizations / takedowns can't
break installs. Original sources kept here for traceability :
- unsloth/Qwen3.5-{2,4,9}B-GGUF                     ( the Qwen3.5 LLM family )
- lm-kit/bge-m3-gguf                                ( embedder )
- gpustack/bge-reranker-v2-m3-GGUF                  ( reranker )
Pro LLM note : bucket is named '-MTP-' ( multi-token prediction variant from
Alibaba ) but the filename inside is plain Qwen3.5-9B-Q4_K_M.gguf — Unsloth
doesn't differentiate filenames between MTP and non-MTP repos , MTP heads are
extra tensors in the same file. Compat check : verify node-llama-cpp's vendored
llama.cpp build understands MTP heads before v0.3.0 release ( if not , file
loads but the MTP speedup is silent-no-op ; if loader strictly validates , the
load fails and we'd swap to the plain Qwen3.5-9B-GGUF bucket ).
sha256 = null means 'size-only verification' ( ±5% tolerance in models.rs ).
Since we own the buckets and files are immutable once pushed , size-only is
acceptable for v0.3.0.
xetHash = the X-Xet-Hash content hash that HF Buckets serve in the response
headers ( 64 hex chars = 256 bits ). VERIFIED 2026-05-24 against sha256sum of
the downloaded bge-m3 file : xetHash ≠ SHA256. Xet uses its own content-
addressed-storage algorithm ( BLAKE3-flavoured ) , not SHA256. The field is
kept as opaque telemetry / source-of-truth for the bucket-side content version
but the Rust downloader ignores it ( serde permits unknown fields ). Real
SHA256s require downloading each file ; bge-m3 was done as the verification ,
the other four LLM SHAs can be backfilled by downloading + computing once.
sizeBytes is the actual on-disk size from the HF Buckets' X-Linked-Size header
( fetched 2026-05-24 ) — accurate to the byte. The downloader still uses
Content-Length at runtime as the authority ; this field drives the wizard UI's
before-the-fact 'total to download' estimate.

models._comment: v0.2.8 : points at UPSTREAM unsloth/Qwen3.5-9B-GGUF ( the NON-MTP variant the eval-säule scored , judge 0.819 ). v0.2.7 shipped the LokLM MTP-bucket by mistake — that's a Mamba/SSM hybrid node-llama-cpp 3.18.1 can't load ( missing tensor blk.N.ssm_conv1d.weight ). sha256 is pinned to the exact file verified-loadable on 2026-05-25 , so an upstream re-quant fails LOUD ( sha mismatch ) rather than shipping another broken model. TODO : mirror this exact file into a LokLM bucket for durability + repoint here.
